pattern: Clamp the circle's center to the nearest point on the rectangle, then check whether the squared distance from center to that clamped point is within the radius squared.
hints: You do not need to check all points inside the rectangle — only the closest point on the rectangle to the circle center matters.
The closest point on an axis-aligned rectangle to an external point is found by independently clamping each coordinate to the rectangle's range.
Clamp cx to [x1,x2] to get px, clamp cy to [y1,y2] to get py; then the shapes overlap if and only if (px-cx)^2 + (py-cy)^2 <= radius^2.

edge_inputs.description: circle tangent to the left edge of rectangle — exactly touching
circle center is above and to the right, does not reach rectangle
circle center is inside the rectangle
circle only touches a corner of the rectangle exactly
circle is just outside a corner — distance slightly too large
radius=1, center exactly on corner of rectangle
circle center on the rectangle boundary (inside edge): always overlaps
circle completely surrounds rectangle but does not intersect if rectangle is outside radius

stress.description: random circles and rectangles across a wide coordinate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    radius = rng.randint(1, 2000)
    xc = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
    yc = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
    x1 = rng.randint(-10000, 9999)
    x2 = rng.randint(x1 + 1, 10000)
    y1 = rng.randint(-10000, 9999)
    y2 = rng.randint(y1 + 1, 10000)
    return [radius, xc, yc, x1, y1, x2, y2]
